content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactually-Guided Prompt Editing: Reducing Social Biases in LLMs via Dynamic Bias-Aware Question Rewriting",
  "problem": "Large language models often propagate social biases and stereotypes due to implicitly encoded associations in their pretraining data. Existing debiasing via prompting methods either require explicit bias recognition by the model or rely on static prompt templates.",
  "motivation": "Prompt-level interventions offer a scalable and user-accessible method for bias reduction in deployed LLMs, but current methods are limited by their inability to dynamically adapt to diverse, context-dependent biases. Addressing this gap is critical as LLMs are increasingly used in sensitive, high-stakes settings where nuanced social biases can have significant real-world impact.",
  "method": "We propose Counterfactually-Guided Prompt Editing (CGPE), a prompting method that programmatically generates and injects minimally perturbed, counterfactual variants of user prompts targeting protected attributes (e.g., gender, ethnicity). For any input prompt, CGPE automatically edits the prompt to create parallel queries that substitute key social variables (e.g., swapping gendered or ethnic identifiers), then uses the pattern of LLM responses to detect likely bias points. The method rewrites the original prompt, in real time, by emphasizing neutrality and explicitly requesting stereotype-avoidant responses informed by counterfactual disparities in generated outputs. Unlike zero-shot self-debiasing or chain-of-thought methods, CGPE leverages dynamic, model-in-the-loop counterfactual rewriting, without requiring training data modification or fixed prompt templates.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will benchmark CGPE on English-language stereotype and bias detection datasets (e.g., StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs) and compare against standard, zero-shot, and chain-of-thought debiasing prompts. Metrics include stereo score, language model accuracy, and human-rated response fairness. Ablation studies will assess the impact of counterfactual editing versus baseline prompts."
}
